channel.title: Dan Patrick Show (Filtered)
channel.description: Daily DP Show episodes without C&R or Best Of clips.
channel.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Making Your Name, Short Arms
item.description:
Dan can’t wait to see which players you’ve never heard of make names for themselves at the NFL Scouting Combine. Plus, DP and the Danettes discuss who has the shortest and longest arms.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – DeMar DeRozan and the Basketball Hall of Fame, Matt Miller
item.description:
DeMar DeRozan is climbing the NBA all-time career scoring list so Dan and the Danettes consider his career and his chances of making the Hall of Fame. Plus, NFL Draft analyst Matt Miller drops by as the NFL Scouting Combine begins.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – “Arm Length” Is the New “Hand Size,” NFL Combine
item.description:
Dan is talking football as the NFL Scouting Combine gets underway. “Arm length” is the latest talking point for QBs at the Combine, replacing the ever popular “hand size” discussions. And they discuss what looks to be a very busy NFL offseason.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Arm-ageddon, Robert Horry
item.description:
The Danettes measure the length of their arms, holding their own NFL Scouting Combine to crown the arm king. Seven-time NBA champion Robert Horry joins the show, and Dan shares his disappointment that “Big Shot Bob” is not in the Hall of Fame, and Horry expresses his feelings about being left out.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Christian Laettner’s Hall of Fame Dilemma, Daniel Jeremiah
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes consider Christian Laettner’s qualifications for the Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame and/or the National Collegiate Basketball Hall of Fame and the difference between the two. NFL analyst Daniel Jeremiah joins the show and talks about the NFL Scouting Combine and what he’s seen from the on-field drills.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – The NFL Combine Is a Guilty Pleasure, Chet vs. Wemby?
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes are into the guilty pleasure that is watching the NFL Scouting Combine. Dan goes over the NFLPA’s team report cards and notices the correlation between high grades and winning. Plus, DP weighs in on the Chet Holmgren vs. Wemby debate that he didn’t even know existed.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – David Montgomery Traded, Jeremy Fowler
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes react to the breaking news that the Detroit Lions have traded RB David Montgomery. And NFL insider Jeremy Fowler joins the show with details of the trade and the offseason action in the league.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Wonderlic Nonsense, Louis Riddick
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes revisit the Wonderlic test, it’s value in NFL scouting, and the embarrassment leveled on those who score poorly. And NFL analyst Louis Riddick drops by to explain how the NFL Scouting Combine can be useful as a tool, not as the primary source for decision making at the NFL Draft.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Combine Clowns, Fire Alarm
item.description:
Dan wonders about the relevance of the information about players coming from the NFL Scouting Combine and predicts there will be players whose stocks will rise and fall because of their performance in Indianapolis instead of what they did on Saturdays. Plus, DP nearly burned down the studios but lived to tell the tale.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Marvin Apologizes, Reggie Miller
item.description:
Danette Marvin Prince and his tiny hands deliver an apology to the fans he may have offended on the video feed during the break. And NBA legend Reggie Miller stops by to talk about all the action on the hardwood and the upcoming college basketball tourney.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Larry Bird Debate, Roger Bennett
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes weigh in on the question regarding Larry Bird and whether he would have still been a superstar if he played in today’s NBA. And soccer analyst Roger Bennett joins the show to talk about the World Cup which starts in just 100 days, and the growth of soccer in the US.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – March Madness Is Coming, Ross Tucker
item.description:
The NFL Scouting Combine is over, and the NBA has hit a lull, so DP turns his attention to college hoops as the NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament is right around the corner. And NFL insider Ross Tucker drops by to chop it up about the NFL Scouting Combine and the NFL offseason.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Reclamation Projects, Team Building
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes hold a snake draft to pick which QB they think will bounce back like Sam Darnold did last season. Plus, Fritzy has the wrong idea about the team building, all-you-can-eat sushi event.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Indoor Golf, Mike Florio
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes discuss indoor golf and the new league that involves some high-profile PGA players. And NFL insider Mike Florio drops by to talk about the upcoming NFL draft, Kyler Murray’s future, and the rest of the NFL offseason action.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Does Kyler Murray Want to Be Great?, Miami of Ohio Deserves to Dance
item.description:
Dan considers where Kyler Murray will play football next season and openly wonders if he really wants to be a great quarterback. And he sincerely hopes the tournament committee will make sure to give Miami of Ohio a bid considering their current 30-0 record, regardless of how they perform in their conference tournament.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Trading Families, Jay Glazer
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes talk about the strange and thorny subjects of trading families and wives between athletes, and Fritzy seems to take too much of an interest in the subject. Fox NFL insider Jay Glazer discusses the relationship between Maxx Crosby and the Raiders, how NFL GMs approach the new generation of transfer portal athletes, plus getting stem cells and ashes with Dan down in Panama.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Michael Silver, Aaron Rodgers’ Future
item.description:
The Athletic sportswriter Michael Silver joins Dan to discuss the state of the Las Vegas Raiders, the struggle from lack of valuable resources, putting Maxx Crosby on the trade block, and where minority owner Tom Brady factors into all this. Stefon Diggs gets cut by the Patriots. Aaron Rodgers’ future is up in the air as he (once again) mulls his future very publicly.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Rams Improve, Lou Holtz Passes
item.description:
The Los Angeles Rams acquired shutdown cornerback Trent McDuffie from the Chiefs, and it has instantly improved their squad and their odds to win a title. Legendary Notre Dame head coach Lou Holtz passed at the age of 89 and Dan fondly remembers his charismatic presence.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 - The Aaron Rodgers Game, Meat Friday descends into chaos
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes discuss the young players across the NBA and who is rising and falling: Payton Pritchard shows why youth is king, but maybe not so much for Dalton Knetcht. The Aaron Rodgers Game tries to predict the future of the most talkative yet most secretive quarterback in the NFL. The Danettes continue to resist Indian food on Meat Friday, callers berate them; Marvin, Fritzy and Seton want chicken nuggets instead. Discussions over how to put on your pants. Geno Smith gets cut from the Raiders.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 - Criticism rachets up for Luka Doncic, Jim Jackson
item.description:
Being under the bright lights of Hollywood has brought new scrutiny to the game of Luka Doncic, and questions to whether he’s living up to his Lakers billing. Dan just doesn’t like the whining. Former NBA star and Fox Sports basketball commentator Jim Jackson weighs in on the open season on Luka, where things are headed with the Lakers, and discusses the stress of the 65-game qualification for NBA MVP and the troubles it has presented for crowning the best player of the year. He also discusses some storylines from college basketball as March Madness looms large. Polls and callers weigh in over food as the rebellion continues over tikka masala and garlic naan.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 - Wemby is changing the game, Ian Rapoport
item.description:
A sudden schism in the Danettes over Indian food on the Meat Friday spread. The Wembanyama Show was on against the Pistons last night, and Dan marvels at the big man’s prowess. A microscope is on Luka Doncic that wasn’t when he was in Dallas. NFL insider Ian Rapoport joins Dan to talk about the strategy of the LA Rams making big splashes in trades, why the Patriots released Stefon Diggs and whether Mac Jones will be available.  Marvin asks the tough question: Buffalo – get to the Super Bowl or win it?

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Manipulating the Madness, Reggie Miller
item.description:
Dan considers how difficult it would be for the NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament selection committee to manipulate the matchups for the tourney. And Hall of Famer Reggie Miller praises Victor Wembanyama's winning mindset and looks back on Kobe using his own move against him in the NBA Finals.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Ross Dellenger, Mike Tannenbaum
item.description:
College football insider Ross Dellenger joins the show after attending the college sports roundtable at the White House and President Trump’s plan to overhaul college athletics. And NFL insider Mike Tannenbaum stops by to weigh in on the Maxx Crosby trade and the deals being proposed around the NFL.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Maxx Crosby Is Happy, NFL Dealmaking Underway
item.description:
Dan talks about Maxx Crosby going to the Baltimore Ravens and NFL teams grabbing proven players instead of relying on the draft. And with the NFL negotiation window officially open, Dan and the Danettes keep you in the loop on all the reported trades and signings.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Emmy Etiquette, NBA Season Is Long
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes discuss their plans in and around their Sports Emmy nomination. And they workshop ideas on how to shorten the NBA season to prevent tanking while still retaining the same revenue.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Luke vs. Owen, Albert Breer
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes get off the rails a little bit as a discussion about Luke and Owen Wilson takes over the conversation. But they get back on track when NFL insider Albert Breer drops by to weigh in on the eventful opening to NFL free agency.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – NFL Signings Galore, SGA Is Unguardable
item.description:
Dan puts a bow on yesterday’s gluttony of signings announced in the NFL yesterday. Plus, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander reached yet another NBA record last night as he dropped 35 on the Nuggets, tying Wilt Chamberlain’s record of 126 games scoring at least 20 points, and you can color DP impressed.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Bam Deserves More Credit, Dianna Russini
item.description:
Dan thinks Bam Adebayo’s 83-point game should be celebrated and doesn’t deserve an asterisk. The Athletic senior NFL insider and the co-host of the Scoop City Podcast with Chase Daniel joins the show and thinks the Ravens rejecting Maxx Crosby seems sus.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Italy Beats Team USA, Jason Jackson
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes discuss the loss for Team USA to Italy in the World Baseball Classic. Miami Heat Radio and TV host Jason Jackson Joins the show to talk about Bam Adebayo's record-breaking game and what it meant for the entire team.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Bam Adebayo Drops 83, Maxx Crosby Out/Trey Hendrickson In
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes dissect Bam Adebayo’s 83-point game last night against the Wizards. And more NFL breaking news hits the Dan Patrick Show as the Baltimore Ravens sign a 4-year deal with DE Trey Hendrickson after backing out of the Maxx Crosby trade.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick
